mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly boosting equity valuations and yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.0% within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger recession fears, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 5.0% in a single month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in industrial production and a decline in real GDP growth indicates a supply-demand mismatch that erodes corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns do not exhibit statistically significant negative skew when both proxies decline concurrently, or joint IC drops below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening monetary cycle, rising short-term rates amplify the negative impact of declining housing starts on consumer wealth, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral when housing starts decline during rate hikes, or interaction term coefficient is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real disposable income and consumer spending directly supports corporate revenue forecasts, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or forward returns show no positive correlation with RSAFS growth.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in industrial production below its 3-month moving average signals supply chain disruptions or demand collapse, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly negative when INDPRO breaches the threshold, or predictive power vanishes in post-2000 data.

mechanism_narrative: When rising unemployment coincides with elevated inflation, it creates stagflationary pressure that forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns do not decline when both unemployment and inflation rise simultaneously, or the interaction term fails to improve out-of-sample R-squared by at least 1%.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-shock recovery phase, a rapid rebound in employment growth validates economic resilience, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises > 150k in a single month
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain flat or negative following large employment gains, or the signal's hit rate drops below 50% in walk-forward testing.
